output.summary: Create PostHero.js with a ResizeObserver that caps .top-gradient max-height to the nearest <main> element height, scoped to .post-hero wrappers. No CSS changes needed -- PostHero already inherits the 150vh height from Hero.css via the shared 'hero' class.

fileChanges.description: Create a frontend JS file with ResizeObserver to cap .top-gradient max-height to <main> height, scoped to .post-hero wrappers.
fileChanges.details: Follow the exact pattern from Hero.js but use the selector '.post-hero .top-gradient' instead of '.hero .top-gradient'. On DOMContentLoaded, query all '.post-hero .top-gradient' elements, find the closest <main> (or fallback to document.querySelector('main')), set gradient.style.maxHeight = main.offsetHeight + 'px', and observe <main> with a ResizeObserver to update dynamically. The webpack glob pattern in webpack.config.js ('...glob.sync("./blocks/**/!(*index).js")') will automatically pick up PostHero.js and bundle it into screen.js -- no manual registration needed.

designDecisions.decision: Use '.post-hero .top-gradient' selector to scope the ResizeObserver to PostHero blocks only
designDecisions.evidence: PostHero.php applies 'post-hero hero' classes to the wrapper via theme_block_props (line 25-31). The .top-gradient div is a direct child (line 35). Using '.post-hero .top-gradient' scopes precisely to PostHero instances and avoids overlap with Hero.js which uses '.hero .top-gradient' (or is being scoped to do so).

designDecisions.decision: No PostHero.css needed -- 150vh height already inherited from Hero.css via shared 'hero' class
designDecisions.evidence: PostHero.php wrapper has class 'post-hero hero' (line 28). Hero.css defines .top-gradient with height: 150vh (already updated from 150vw). The shared 'hero' class ensures PostHero inherits these gradient styles. No duplication needed.

designDecisions.decision: Use standalone PostHero.js file (not inline PHP script) for the ResizeObserver
designDecisions.evidence: Hero.js uses the same standalone file approach. webpack.config.js glob pattern ('...glob.sync("./blocks/**/!(*index).js")') automatically bundles any .js file in block directories into screen.js. This avoids PHP function validator issues with JavaScript identifiers and follows the established QuickNavigation.js / Hero.js pattern.
